en_body:
Spring...silver silken buds plumply speckle and burst forth
Radiant reaches of many colors open tulips
As far as eyes can see the fields are velvet
O flowers...whom do you love, blooming with such living force drawing one into colors??
Your sky...fields and gardens of a thousand purples
Paths weave through, leading Love to her throne
You sit silent within the orderly house,
I dream to see your arms among a thousand flowers:
Upon my dream-road still inclines one lonely shadow
Far and wide drift birds with wearied wings
Reaching beyond measure to the ends of Spring fields
Seeking You among ten thousand shades and colors
And the little dream stops within the little house:

Does Spring ripple upon your cool skin?
Flowers still blossom fresh though You turn no eye to see,
Spring awakens in my body the ache of remembrance
Watching flowers offer crimson colors: stirs the Love!
Silently entrusting to the flowers a manifesting soul
To draw your eyes and stir your heart:
Please remind You of one Love so silent
O Spring: awaken Love
Within your heart, where warm blood seems tender!
note: The poem's tulip fields and gardens evoke a northern spring landscape while Love is enthroned as both human passion and sacred presence.
